what: Official GGUF Parsing and Typed Storage Views
title: Official GGUF Parsing and Typed Storage Views
text: Rgguf reads GGUF model files - format used llama.cpp store large language model tensors metadata - exposes tensors decoded Rfmalloc arrays, copied native codecs, zero-copy read-views original model mapping. GGUF parsing writing use official implementation GGML, carried sibling Rggml package. Rgguf maps tensor data read-owns second GGUF parser.

title: Official GGUF Parsing and Typed Storage Views
text: three storage paths: = "numeric": R allocates destination Rfmalloc::create_fmalloc_matrix()/create_fmalloc_array(), returns properly classed, file-backed ALTREP object - Rfmalloc’s Ops/matrix-product dispatch already works . Rggml’s official parser locates tensor read-GGUF mapping. Native code writes dequantized values directly destination’s REAL() payload (ALTREP Dataptr exposes file-backed fmalloc memory directly - extra copy back ordinary R vector). = "native": encoded bytes copied owned fmalloc storage. useful GGUF file may disappear payload needs independent lifetime. = "view": tensor borrows exact span original read-mapping. view keeps mapping alive gives codecs GGML pointer, model loading performs weight copy. GGML/GGUF tensors store dim[0] fastest-varying (contiguous) dimension. R arrays column-major (first dimension varies fastest), dim[0] maps directly onto R’s first dimension: dim(gguf_tensor(x, name)) == c(dim[0], dim[1], ...), transposition needed. verified dedicated round-trip test (inst/tinytest/test_gguf_roundtrip.R).

title: Official GGUF Parsing and Typed Storage Views
Official GGUF Parsing and Typed Storage Views
text: example uses local() .exit() cleanup stays scoped snippet. code, keep runtime handle long arrays live call Rfmalloc::cleanup_fmalloc() finished.
code: library(Rgguf)  local({   gguf_file <- tempfile(fileext = ".gguf")   alloc_file <- tempfile(fileext = ".bin")   rt <- Rfmalloc::open_fmalloc(alloc_file)   on.exit({     Rfmalloc::cleanup_fmalloc(rt)     unlink(c(gguf_file, alloc_file))   }, add = TRUE)    # Build a small example GGUF file (gguf_write_tensors() only emits F32   # tensors; it exists mainly to make round-trip tests possible without   # shipping binary fixtures).   set.seed(42)   gguf_write_tensors(gguf_file,     tensors = list(       w1 = matrix(rnorm(100 * 50), nrow = 100, ncol = 50),       w2 = matrix(rnorm(50 * 30), nrow = 50, ncol = 30)     ),     metadata = list(name = "example-model", version = 1)   )    # Inspect metadata and the tensor directory without reading any payload.   str(gguf_metadata(gguf_file))   print(gguf_tensors(gguf_file))    # Read tensors into Rfmalloc-backed matrices sharing one backing file.   mats <- gguf_import(gguf_file, runtime = rt)   print(Rfmalloc::is_fmalloc_vector(mats$w1))    # Matrix products dispatch through Rfmalloc and stay file-backed.   product <- mats$w1 %*% mats$w2   print(dim(product))   print(Rfmalloc::is_fmalloc_vector(product))   print(max(abs(product - as.matrix(mats$w1[, ]) %*% as.matrix(mats$w2[, ])))) }) #> List of 2 #>  $ name   : chr "example-model" #>  $ version: num 1 #>   name type n_dims    dims n_elements nbytes offset #> 1   w1  f32      2 100, 50       5000  20000    192 #> 2   w2  f32      2  50, 30       1500   6000  20192 #> [1] TRUE #> [1] 100  30 #> [1] TRUE #> [1] 0

what: Native typed tensors and borrowed views
title: Official GGUF Parsing and Typed Storage Views
text: gguf_tensor(= "native") skips dequantization import: tensor’s raw GGUF payload copied fmalloc storage original density (4.5 bits/weight q4_k) returned Rfmalloc::fmalloc_tensor. Matrix products dense operands decode payload bounded, block-aligned panels streamed BLAS dgemm, full double representation never materialized. Rgguf registers GGML-backed codecs q4_0, q4_1, q5_0, q5_1, q8_0, q2_k, q3_k, q4_k, q5_k, q6_k; f32, f16, bf16, f64 codecs built Rfmalloc. gguf_tensor(= "view") typed-tensor interface copy payload. inference path: Rllm points GGML directly borrowed bytes. Rfmalloc::fmalloc_storage_advise() can express sequential, prefetch, release intentions view --core schedulers.
code: local({   gguf_file <- tempfile(fileext = ".gguf")   alloc_file <- tempfile(fileext = ".bin")   rt <- Rfmalloc::open_fmalloc(alloc_file)   on.exit({     Rfmalloc::cleanup_fmalloc(rt)     unlink(c(gguf_file, alloc_file))   }, add = TRUE)    set.seed(7)   gguf_write_tensors(gguf_file, list(     w = matrix(rnorm(256 * 64), nrow = 256, ncol = 64)   ))    ten <- gguf_tensor(gguf_file, "w", runtime = rt, as = "native")   print(ten)    x <- matrix(rnorm(8 * 256), nrow = 8)   y <- x %*% ten # decoded panel-by-panel inside dgemm   print(dim(y))   print(Rfmalloc::is_fmalloc_vector(y))    w_f64 <- gguf_tensor(gguf_file, "w", runtime = rt)   print(max(abs(y[] - (x %*% w_f64)[]))) }) #> <fmalloc_tensor f32 [256 x 64], 65536 payload bytes> #> [1]  8 64 #> [1] TRUE #> [1] 0

title: Official GGUF Parsing and Typed Storage Views
text: gguf_tensor() gguf_import() use GGML’s authoritative decoder file’s declared type. Plain numeric types widened directly. Quantized types decode fixed 64 KiB float scratch caller-owned double destination, independent tensor size.

title: Official GGUF Parsing and Typed Storage Views
text: gguf_open() parses metadata maps tensor bytes read-. readable model file sufficient; Rgguf never modifies .

title: Official GGUF Parsing and Typed Storage Views
text: GPL (>= 2). Rgguf contains vendored third-party source. Rggml documents pinned official GGML sources provides.

what: Rgguf: Official GGUF parsing and typed storage views — Rgguf-package
title: Rgguf: Official GGUF parsing and typed storage views — Rgguf-package
text: Rgguf reads 'GGUF' model files, file format used 'llama.cpp' project store large language model tensors metadata, exposes tensors decoded Rfmalloc arrays, owned native-codec copies, borrowed read-views. Parsing, writing, quantized decoding use official GGUF type-traits implementation carried sibling Rggml package.

title: Rgguf: Official GGUF parsing and typed storage views — Rgguf-package
text: gguf_open Open GGUF file return context handle. gguf_metadata Read metadata key-value pairs. gguf_tensors List tensor directory data frame. gguf_tensor Read, copy, borrow one tensor. gguf_import Apply storage choice several tensors. gguf_write_tensors Write minimal GGUF file (F32 tensors), mainly round-trip testing.

title: Rgguf: Official GGUF parsing and typed storage views — Rgguf-package
text: Allocation happens R side: tensor destinations created Rfmalloc::create_fmalloc_matrix()/create_fmalloc_array(), returns properly classed, file-backed ALTREP object Rfmalloc's full Ops/matrix-product dispatch already working. Native code ever fills destination place (dequantizing goes); never allocates R vectors tensor size .

title: Rgguf: Official GGUF parsing and typed storage views — Rgguf-package
text: GGUF files mapped read-. Metadata tensor geometry parsed Rggml's official GGUF implementation. Numeric import decodes GGML bounded chunks fmalloc destination. Native import copies encoded bytes owned fmalloc storage. View import borrows original mapped span.

title: Rgguf: Official GGUF parsing and typed storage views — Rgguf-package
text: Maintainer: Sounkou Mahamane Toure sounkoutoure@gmail.com

title: Import GGUF Tensors — gguf_import
text: Reads tensors GGUF file named list Rfmalloc-backed matrices/arrays, dequantizing needed. thin convenience wrapper around repeated gguf_tensor() calls shares single open file handle across .

title: Import GGUF Tensors — gguf_import
code: gguf_import(   path_or_ctx,   tensors = NULL,   runtime = NULL,   as = c("numeric", "native", "view") )

title: Import GGUF Tensors — gguf_import
text: path_or_ctx Either path GGUF file, "gguf_ctx" object returned gguf_open(). tensors Optional character vector tensor names import. NULL (default), every tensor file imported. runtime Optional Rfmalloc runtime handle passed every gguf_tensor() call, imported tensors share backing file. NULL, Rfmalloc's default-runtime resolution used tensor. Passed gguf_tensor(): "numeric" dequantizes fmalloc double matrices/arrays, "native" copies encoded bytes fmalloc storage, "view" borrows original read-GGUF spans.

title: Import GGUF Tensors — gguf_import
text: named list tensors, order requested (file order, tensors NULL). Numeric native imports fmalloc allocations; views read-spans original GGUF mapping.

title: Import GGUF Tensors — gguf_import
code: tmp <- tempfile(fileext = ".gguf") gguf_write_tensors(tmp, list(     w1 = matrix(as.double(1:12), nrow = 4, ncol = 3),     w2 = matrix(as.double(1:6), nrow = 3, ncol = 2) )) rt <- Rfmalloc::open_fmalloc(tempfile(fileext = ".bin")) mats <- gguf_import(tmp, runtime = rt) prod <- mats$w1 %*% mats$w2 Rfmalloc::is_fmalloc_vector(prod) #> [1] TRUE unlink(tmp)

title: Read GGUF Metadata — gguf_metadata
text: Reads every metadata key-value pair GGUF file named R list.

title: Read GGUF Metadata — gguf_metadata
Read GGUF Metadata — gguf_metadata
text: x Either path GGUF file, "gguf_ctx" object returned gguf_open().

title: Read GGUF Metadata — gguf_metadata
text: named list metadata values, file order.

title: Read GGUF Metadata — gguf_metadata
text: Scalar values converted closest native R type (integer 8/16/32-bit signed unsigned--int-safe integers, numeric 32-bit unsigned 64-bit integers/floats, logical booleans, character strings). Arrays supported scalar type become R vector type. metadata value type package represent (none current GGUF spec, check defensive) returned NULL keeping name, rather raising error.

title: Read GGUF Metadata — gguf_metadata
code: tmp <- tempfile(fileext = ".gguf") gguf_write_tensors(tmp, list(w = matrix(1:6 + 0.5, nrow = 2)),     metadata = list(name = "example", version = 1)) gguf_metadata(tmp) #> $name #> [1] "example" #>  #> $version #> [1] 1 #>  unlink(tmp)

title: Open a GGUF File — gguf_open
text: Opens 'GGUF' model file returns lightweight context handle used gguf_metadata(), gguf_tensors(), gguf_tensor(), gguf_import(). Metadata tensor directory parsed Rggml's official GGUF implementation. Tensor bytes mapped read-unmapped automatically returned object garbage collected, earlier call gguf_import()/friends plain file path (open close short-lived context internally).

title: Open a GGUF File — gguf_open
Open a GGUF File — gguf_open
text: path Character string giving path GGUF file.

title: Open a GGUF File — gguf_open
text: object class "gguf_ctx": external pointer underlying parser context, finalizer closes .

title: Open a GGUF File — gguf_open
code: tmp <- tempfile(fileext = ".gguf") gguf_write_tensors(tmp, list(w = matrix(1:6 + 0.5, nrow = 2))) ctx <- gguf_open(tmp) gguf_tensors(ctx) #>   name type n_dims dims n_elements nbytes offset #> 1    w  f32      2 2, 3          6     24     96 unlink(tmp)

what: Read and Dequantize a GGUF Tensor — gguf_tensor
title: Read and Dequantize a GGUF Tensor — gguf_tensor
text: Reads single named tensor GGUF file, dequantizing needed, fresh Rfmalloc-backed ALTREP matrix array doubles.

title: Read and Dequantize a GGUF Tensor — gguf_tensor
code: gguf_tensor(x, name, runtime = NULL, as = c("numeric", "native", "view"))

title: Read and Dequantize a GGUF Tensor — gguf_tensor
text: x Either path GGUF file, "gguf_ctx" object returned gguf_open(). name Tensor name, appears gguf_tensors()'s name column. runtime Optional Rfmalloc runtime handle (see Rfmalloc::open_fmalloc()). NULL, Rfmalloc's default-runtime resolution used. "numeric" (default) dequantizes whole tensor fmalloc double matrix/array. "native" copies tensor's raw, still-encoded payload fmalloc storage returns Rfmalloc::create_fmalloc_tensor() typed tensor: keeps GGUF storage density (e.g. 4.5 bits/weight q4_k) decoded bounded panels used matrix products. "view" returns typed tensor without copying: borrows exact read-span original GGUF mapping keeps mapping alive. Native mode requires two dimensions; view mode accepts rank. require registered Rfmalloc codec.

title: Read and Dequantize a GGUF Tensor — gguf_tensor
text: = "numeric", Rfmalloc-backed ALTREP matrix (tensor 2 dimensions) array (otherwise) doubles, dim() equal tensor's GGUF dimensions c(dim[0], dim[1], ...) order. = "native" "view", fmalloc_tensor dims.

title: Read and Dequantize a GGUF Tensor — gguf_tensor
text: GGUF tensor dimensions stored dim[0] fastest-varying (contiguous) dimension. R arrays column-major, .e. first dimension varies fastest, dim[0] maps directly onto R's first dimension transposition needed: dim(result) == c(dim[0], dim[1], ...). See inst/tinytest/test_gguf_roundtrip.R test verifies mapping writing known matrices/arrays reading back.

title: Read and Dequantize a GGUF Tensor — gguf_tensor
code: tmp <- tempfile(fileext = ".gguf") gguf_write_tensors(tmp, list(w = matrix(1:6 + 0.5, nrow = 2))) rt <- Rfmalloc::open_fmalloc(tempfile(fileext = ".bin")) w <- gguf_tensor(tmp, "w", runtime = rt) w #>      [,1] [,2] [,3] #> [1,]  1.5  3.5  5.5 #> [2,]  2.5  4.5  6.5 #> attr(,"class") #> [1] "fmalloc_matrix" "matrix"         "fmalloc"        "numeric"        Rfmalloc::is_fmalloc_vector(w) #> [1] TRUE unlink(tmp)

title: List GGUF Tensors — gguf_tensors
text: Lists tensor directory GGUF file data frame, without reading dequantizing tensor payload.

title: List GGUF Tensors — gguf_tensors
List GGUF Tensors — gguf_tensors
text: x Either path GGUF file, "gguf_ctx" object returned gguf_open().

title: List GGUF Tensors — gguf_tensors
text: data frame one row per tensor columns: name Tensor name. type GGUF tensor type name, e.g. "f32", "q4_k". n_dims Number dimensions. dims list column; dims[[]] integer vector tensor 's dimensions, GGUF-order (dim[0] first, fastest-varying/ contiguous dimension, gguf_tensor() maps R's first, column-major dimension). n_elements Total number scalar elements. nbytes Size tensor's raw (possibly quantized) -disk representation, bytes. offset Byte offset tensor data start file.

title: List GGUF Tensors — gguf_tensors
code: tmp <- tempfile(fileext = ".gguf") gguf_write_tensors(tmp, list(w = matrix(1:6 + 0.5, nrow = 2), b = 1:3 + 0.5)) gguf_tensors(tmp) #>   name type n_dims dims n_elements nbytes offset #> 1    w  f32      2 2, 3          6     24    128 #> 2    b  f32      1    3          3     12    160 unlink(tmp)

what: Write a Minimal GGUF File — gguf_write_tensors
title: Write a Minimal GGUF File — gguf_write_tensors
text: Writes named list numeric vectors/matrices/arrays GGUF file 32-bit floating point (f32) tensors, optional simple metadata. minimal writer, primarily meant build small GGUF fixtures package's round-trip tests without shipping binary test fixtures, exported since also useful producing test fixtures GGUF consumers.

title: Write a Minimal GGUF File — gguf_write_tensors
Write a Minimal GGUF File — gguf_write_tensors
text: path Character string giving output file path. tensors non-empty named list numeric vectors, matrices, arrays. Names become tensor names must unique non-empty. metadata named list metadata key-value pairs write. Values may non-missing character vectors single non-missing numeric values. Character vectors length one written GGUF string arrays; numeric values written 64-bit floats (FLOAT64). Defaults list() (metadata).

title: Write a Minimal GGUF File — gguf_write_tensors
text: path, invisibly.

title: Write a Minimal GGUF File — gguf_write_tensors
text: Tensor dimensions taken object's dim() (length() plain vector, written 1-dimensional tensor) stored GGUF dim[0]-fastest-varying order directly R's column-major storage, gguf_tensor()/gguf_import() read back exactly matrix/array wrote (see inst/tinytest/test_gguf_roundtrip.R). Existing files path silently overwritten.

title: Write a Minimal GGUF File — gguf_write_tensors
code: tmp <- tempfile(fileext = ".gguf") gguf_write_tensors(tmp,     tensors = list(weight = matrix(1:6 + 0.5, nrow = 2)),     metadata = list(name = "example-model", version = 1) ) gguf_tensors(tmp) #>     name type n_dims dims n_elements nbytes offset #> 1 weight  f32      2 2, 3          6     24    160 unlink(tmp)

title: Rgguf 0.1.0 (unreleased)
text: Added gguf_tensor(= "view") gguf_import(= "view"). view borrows tensor’s exact read-span original GGUF mapping, keeps mapping alive, enters Rfmalloc/GGML compute without copying encoded payload second file. Removed partial vendored gguflib parser. Rggml now carries GGML’s official gguf.cpp; Rgguf uses indexed metadata, tensor directory, writer, type-traits decoders, mapping tensor bytes read-. Numeric decode uses bounded scratch, ten common quantized codecs registered rather split Rgguf Rllm. gguf_tensor(= "native") copies raw encoded payload needs registered Rfmalloc codec. = "numeric" decodes GGML’s reference implementation. Removed inherited Q4_K scale-indexing bug deleting independent decoder. Codec outputs pinned GGML test_gguf_codec_ggml_ref.R. Added native typed imports: gguf_tensor(= "native") (gguf_import(= "native")) copy 2-d tensor’s raw GGUF payload fmalloc storage original density (e.g. 4.5 bits/weight q4_k) return Rfmalloc::fmalloc_tensor, decoded bounded panels inside matrix products. Registers GGML-backed codecs q4_0, q4_1, q5_0, q5_1, q8_0, q2_k, q3_k, q4_k, q5_k, q6_k. Initial release. Added gguf_open() parse map GGUF file read-return gguf_ctx handle closing finalizer. Added gguf_metadata() read metadata key-value pairs (scalars arrays supported GGUF value types) named R list. Added gguf_tensors() list tensor directory (name, type, number dimensions, dimensions, element count, byte size, file offset) data frame, without reading tensor payload. Added gguf_tensor() read dequantize single named tensor directly Rfmalloc-backed, file-backed ALTREP matrix/array doubles: R allocates destination via Rfmalloc::create_fmalloc_matrix()/create_fmalloc_array(), native code fills place, ordinary R-sized copy tensor ever materialized R heap. Added gguf_import() read tensors file named list Rfmalloc-backed matrices/arrays sharing one open file handle. Added gguf_write_tensors(), minimal GGUF writer (F32 tensors plus simple string/numeric metadata), primarily build small GGUF fixtures package’s round-trip tests without shipping binary fixtures. Verified (dedicated round-trip test) GGUF’s dim[0] (fastest-varying/contiguous dimension) maps directly onto R’s first, column-major dimension, tensor dimensions need transposition: dim(gguf_tensor(x, name)) == c(dim[0], dim[1], ...). Numeric decoding uses GGML’s storage-type implementation plain quantized tensors. Documented (README, inst/smoke_test.R) discovered Rfmalloc 0.1.0 limitation, Rgguf bug: %*%/crossprod/tcrossprod fmalloc-backed operands >= 64 elements currently fail operand passed R closure call (effectively always), due generic R-core ALTREP attribute-wrapping behavior Rfmalloc unwrap. gguf_tensor()/gguf_import() unaffected size.
